summary: 1st Open Call round – Supporting SMEs in the Textile Sector towards Digital and Green Transition
content: 1st Open Call round – Supporting SMEs in the Textile Sector towards Digital and Green Transition
citation: Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/47108154
metadata.title: ['Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery']
metadata.caName: ['1st Open Call round – Supporting SMEs in the Textile Sector towards Digital and Green Transition']
metadata.description:
['

Link to the online Application Portal : https://ec.europa.eu/eusurvey/runner/FutureProofTextiles-1st-OpenCall

Open Call objective: Empowering 20 SMEs in the textile sector to successfully navigate the "twin transitions" of digital transformation and green sustainability.

Support: Beyond the €60,000 co-financing grant (50 000 EUR – fixed lump Sum Grant, 10 000 EUR – required self-funding by SME), FutureProofTextiles offers extensive capacity-building and networking resources to help build a connected SME ecosystem:

  1. Tailored Training: An average of 40 hours per SME focusing on digital transformation, circular economy principles, and sustainable production.
  2. Dedicated Mentoring: Custom technical consulting and mentoring sessions for up to 6 selected SMEs based on specific needs.
  3. Open-Access Materials: Production of 100 hours of scalable training curricula and best-practice guides for the broader textile sector.
  4. Cross-Border Networking: Facilitation of international collaboration to foster an innovative textile ecosystem.

(Further details regarding these services will be shared with the selected SMEs.)

Who can apply:

Company size

  1. Applicants must fall under the category of SME, according toEuropean Commission Recommendation 2003/361/EC*
  2. Partnerships Clusters and intermediary organisations are not eligible even if they are SMEs.
  3. Each applicant should represent a single SME, not a consortia of multiple SMEs.

Industry

  1. Applicants must be SMEs operating in the textile value chain. SMEs from complementary sectors (e.g., tech, machinery) are also eligible, provided their proposed innovation directly contributes to the textile value chain. Activities must align with a relevant NACE code (or national equivalent), such as:
  2. Textile manufacturing: C13.1, C13.2, C13.3, C13.9
  3. Apparel manufacturing: C14.1, C14.3
  4. Textile-based products and technical textiles: C15.12, C22.19
  5. Textile recycling and circularity: C38
  6. Digital and innovative solutions for the textile sector: J62, J63
  7. Manufacture of machinery for textile, apparel and leather production:C 28.94

Location

  1. Only SMEs legally established in countries participating in the Single Market Programme (SMP) are eligible to apply. This includes EU Member States and any other country formally associated with the SMP. A full list of participating countries is published here: List of participating countries in the Single Market Programme (SMP)

Language

  1. The applicant SME must have at least one staff member who speaks English and will be actively involved in the implementation process, handling all communication needs with the consortium.

Submission Rules:

Platform

  1. Proposal submitted online on the EU Survey Platform. Any other means will not be accepted. Application Form

Language

  1. Proposals must be written in English. Submissions done in any other language will not be evaluated.

Submission Limit

  1. Applicant SMEs may submit up to two proposals per Open Call round (one under Green Transition and one under Digital Innovations). Despite this, each SME is eligible to receive a maximum of one grant award across the entire program.

Proposal Requirements

  1. The proposal must demonstrate a Technology Readiness Level (TRL) of 5 or higher.

Compliance & Ethics

Applicants must confirm their full compliance with all rules, including absence of double funding, and conflict of interest, by reviewing and signing the mandatory forms included in the application:

  1. Funding eligibility and absence of conflict of interest declaration form
  2. SME and Eligibility declaration form

Step by Step Guide

Applicants may submit one proposal for a Green Transition project and one for a Digital Innovation project. Please note that a separate application must be submitted for each.

Step 1: Verify Eligibility

Review the eligibility requirements thoroughly to ensure your organization and project qualify for funding.

Step 2: Concept & Budgeting

  1. Check out “Exhaustive List of Eligible Activities and Eligible Costs” document.
  2. Check out “Needs of the SMEs in the Textile Sector” document, which helps the brainstorming process.
  3. Brainstorm your proposal. When brainstorming, keep in mind that the project implementation period is 9months.
  4. Compose an initial draft, and develop a comprehensive budget based on eligible costs. Clearly define your objectives by establishing specific milestones.

Step 3: Draft Responses

  1. Prepare your answers for each section of the “Standardized Application Form Template” document, before starting the online submission.

Step 4: Prepare Mandatory Documents

Complete and sign the following required forms:

  1. Complete the “Tables with Activities, Budget, Milestones, and Risks” document. Please note that milestones may be subject to negotiation if the proposal is selected, and applicants will not be evaluated based on the proposed milestones
  2. Fill in the “SME and Eligibility Declaration form”.
  3. Fill in the “Funding eligibility and absence of conflict of interest declaration form”.

Step 5: Complete the Online Form

Access the online portal – EU Survey to input your responses. We recommend using a stable internet connection to prevent data loss.

Step 6: Upload Attachments

Attach all mandatory documents from Step 4 into their respective upload fields within the form.

All necessary documents are available for downloading through the Application portal and through the relevant posts in projects LindeIn and ECCP page.

profile.clustercollaboration.eu/profile/cluster-partnership-initiative/c9fcd533-bf23-4b23-bd18-adb2e57b37a4

https://www.linkedin.com/company/future-proof-textiles  

You can also contact: fpt.eu.helpdesk@gmail.com



']

summary: UPSTREAM Open Call 2: Prevention & Zero‑Waste
content: UPSTREAM Open Call 2: Prevention & Zero‑Waste
citation: Prevent and eliminate litter, plastics and microplastics: Innovative solutions for waste-free European rivers. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/44799917
metadata.title: ['Prevent and eliminate litter, plastics and microplastics: Innovative solutions for waste-free European rivers']
metadata.description:
['

Eligible activities (exhaustive list for the calls)

Activity focus: Prevention and Zero-Waste on rivers

Projects must demonstrably support UPSTREAM objectives through: 1) Monitoring & digitalisation of plastics/MP in rivers & tributaries; 2) Prevention & zero‑waste measures (e.g., substitution with bio‑based/biodegradable alternatives, upstream interventions); 3) Removal/collection & recovery of litter, plastics and microplastics in freshwater systems; 4) Recycling/valorisation pilots for captured plastics (incl. pre‑treatments that enable recycling); 5) Nature‑based solutions & restoration in riparian and urban water systems; 6) Citizen science & co‑creation (living labs, behaviour‑change campaigns) that directly support the above; 7) Open data & knowledge sharing with UPSTREAM’s platforms.

Activities sought. Substitution & procurement pilots; zero‑waste supply chain measures; behaviour‑change and producer responsibility pilots; enabling policies; monitoring impacts

Not eligible: Basic research; routine municipal operations; activities completed before grant start; double‑funded activities; purchases unrelated to project tasks; measures with significant negative environmental impacts.

Deliverables, KPIs & reporting (for grantees)

Mandatory deliverables: - D1: Kick‑off package (work plan, risk & ethics checklist, data plan, comms plan) – M1; - D2: Mid‑term update (evidence of activities, preliminary results) – mid‑point; - D3: Final pack (technical report, datasets, photos/videos, replication & exploitation plan, communication assets) – final month.

Illustrative KPIs (select per project): capture efficiency; tonnes/items collected; MP concentration trends; citizens engaged; policy changes enacted; % bio‑based substitutions; valorisation outputs; open datasets published.

Visibility. Use MISSION OCEAN/EU/UPSTREAM logos and funding statement on all outputs; provide consented media assets; follow project visual ID.

Open data. Publish non‑sensitive results to UPSTREAM platforms/Portal as applicable.

']

summary: Support for the Integration of Post-Quantum Cryptography into Application Platforms
content: Support for the Integration of Post-Quantum Cryptography into Application Platforms
citation: Deploying The Network of National Coordination Centres with Member States. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46786367
metadata.title: ['Deploying The Network of National Coordination Centres with Member States']
metadata.caName: ['Support for the Integration of Post-Quantum Cryptography into Application Platforms']
metadata.description:
['

Project scope

The objective of the call is to support all categories of companies that integrate post-quantum cryptography into application platforms. The supported projects should help organisations prepare for future quantum-related cybersecurity risks by analysing existing cryptographic mechanisms, identifying suitable applications for migration, documenting application architecture and data flows, designing the replacement of cryptographic algorithms, and preparing a migration plan.

Mandatory activities include risk analysis based on the assessment of cryptographic mechanisms, identification and selection of an application suitable for migration to post-quantum cryptographic mechanisms, description of the application architecture, documentation of implemented cryptographic mechanisms, proposal for replacing cryptographic algorithms, and preparation of a project plan.

Optional activities may include implementation of post-quantum cryptography, testing and updating the application architecture. If optional activities are selected, all optional activities must be implemented, and the applicant must demonstrate sufficient internal or external capacity to carry out the implementation.

']

summary: SMART ERA 2nd Open Call for Followers Micro-Pilots
content: SMART ERA 2nd Open Call for Followers Micro-Pilots
citation: Smart solutions for smart rural communities: empowering rural communities and smart villages to innovate for societal change. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/44088443
metadata.title: ['Smart solutions for smart rural communities: empowering rural communities and smart villages to innovate for societal change']
metadata.caName: ['SMART ERA 2nd Open Call for Followers Micro-Pilots']
metadata.description:
['

SMART ERA is an EU-funded project that aims to transform rural areas by addressing their socio-economic and environmental challenges through advanced technology and innovative solutions.

SMART ERA impacts will span across technology, governance, business, society, and policymaking, fostering sustainable development and growth.

The six (6) Pilot Regions leading the way are:

  1. Val di Sole/Trentino, Italy
  2. Tramuntana/Sóller, Spain 
  3. Northern Ostrobothnia, Finland
  4. Trebinje/East Herzegovina, Bosnia and Herzegovina
  5. Šmarje-Padna, Slovenia, Italy, Croatia
  6. Devetaki Plateau, Bulgaria

They will become smart villages through the application of Smart Innovation Packages (SIPs) co-designed and validated with local communities. Joint efforts can revitalize rural communities, combat depopulation, and create resilient, vibrant societies.

The SMART ERA consortium brings together 25 organisations from 10 countries, counting with the adequate resources for achieving the project objectives. 

With this 2nd Open Call for Followers Micro-Pilots, SMART ERA aims to transfer the project\'s results, methodology, and validation strategies to four (4) new "Follower Regions", engaging new local communities to prove the replicability and impact potential of the SMART ERA approach.


The SMART ERA 2nd Open Call for Followers Micro-Pilots will select up to 4 Proposals to establish 4 Followers Micro-Pilots. These Micro-Pilots will replicate the SMART ERA methodology by identifying regional needs and co-designing new Smart Innovation Packages (SIP). 

The total maximum funding for one Follower Micro-Pilot depends on the application format: 

  1. If applying as a Consortium total lump sum up to €90,000, distributed as follows:
  2. Community Activator: Up to €30,000.
  3. Pilot Orchestrator (Technical Provider): Up to €60,000.
  4. If applying as an Individual (covering both roles):
  5. Total lump sum: Up to €60,000.

Each team selected under the SMART ERA 2nd Open Call will join a 12-month Support Programme with the mentoring from SMART ERA Technical Partners. 

The Support Programme includes the following stages:

  1. Stage 1: On-boarding Stage, during which the beneficiary should deliver an Individual Mentoring Plan (including planned activities, KPIs, milestones and budget). The duration of this stage is up to 2 months.
  2. Stage 2: Mobilisation Stage, during which the Report on Community Engagement should be delivered. The duration of this stage is up to 3 months.
  3. Stage 3: Deployment Stage, which should lead to a Technical Solution Completed. The duration of this stage is up to 7 months.

At the end of the Support Programme, the selected teams will have developed:

  1. A design for a Smart Innovation Package
  2. A technological solution, as the center of the SIP, developed and tested.


']

metadata.description:
['

The NEB Academy | Fashion Shift call supports the transformation of the European fashion industry by funding collaborative consortia that develop and scale circular, regenerative and responsible business models grounded in the New European Bauhaus values of sustainability, inclusion and beauty.

Activities may focus on different parts of the fashion value chain, including design, material sourcing, production, distribution and end-of-life management, and should generate measurable environmental, social, societal and market impact.

metadata.title: ['Higher Education Initiative Business Plan 2026']
metadata.description:
['

The HEIPowered Startup Pitch Competition is a platform to continue supporting startups that have emerged or received backing by projects funded by the EIT Higher Education Initiative since 2021, providing follow-up opportunities for startups beyond the initial project lifecycle.

Its main objective is to further accelerate the growth of HEI-supported startups, strengthen their market readiness, scalability, and long-term sustainability, and contribute to a stronger entrepreneurship and innovation ecosystem in Europe. To achieve this objective, the Call focuses on:

• Advancing innovation pathways in startups that have reached a proof of concept, prototype, or minimum viable product (MVP) stage, helping them progress towards market deployment.

• Empowering startups with well-structured teams that possess the necessary skills, experience, and competencies to effectively implement and scale their projects.

• Promoting diversity and inclusion by fostering gender-balanced and diverse startups, with a strong encouragement for women-led startups and active participation of women in leadership and decision-making roles.

• Providing startups with opportunities to increase their visibility, connect with stakeholders, and access further development and scaling support.

• Promoting and strengthening the EIT Higher Education Initiative’s Community with new startup members and opportunities for them.

']

summary: NGI Fediversity open call (2026-08F)
content: NGI Fediversity open call (2026-08F)
citation: Pilots for the Next Generation Internet (IA). Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45635989
metadata.title: ['Pilots for the Next Generation Internet (IA)']
metadata.description:
['

Note that this programme is building a hosting stack based on the Nix functional package manager. if your project is not related to Nix or to high availability hosting, we suggest you submit to another open call from NLnet such as Restack. Please also note that there is a policy on the use of AI, and that any submission involving AI involves providing a prompt log. All generated content should be marked as such, even if subsequently edited.

Our software supply chains are a silent miracle, the result of millions of hours spent every year packaging, compiling and distributing software. From this vast firehose of bits come the apps and services we depend on every day. Subsequently, billions of hours are spent by users on dealing with everything from simple updates to complex system administration. This vast effort is necessary to keep oneself abreast of the never-ending supply of potential vulnerabilities that arrives with just a slight delay. Users that fail to responsively track new releases of every deployed service and each of its dependencies, are bound to land themselves or others in trouble sooner rather than later.

Deploying internet based services is much tougher than we can afford. No matter how user friendly or well-written software is, enabling the community at large to maintain internet services by themselves at scale has proven to be consistently challenging. Achieving high availability scenario\'s is even more of a dark art. No wonder, people just give in and surrender their data.

Fediversity is a comprehensive effort to bring easy-to-use, hosted online services with service portability and personal freedom at their core to everyone. It wants to provide everyone with high-quality, secure IT systems for everyday use. Without tracking, without exploitation, in a way that runs everywhere and scales effortlessly. Fediversity is based on NixOS, a disruptive Linux distribution with a unique approach to package and configuration management. Built on top of the Nix package manager, NixOS is completely declarative, makes upgrading systems reliable, and has many other advantages. Because it is reproducible, it is ideally suited for complex deployment scenario\'s where consistent behaviour, stability and configurability matter.

One such "complex" deployment scenario is running state-of-the-art services for the Fediverse, like PeerTube, Mastodon, Owncast or Lemmy — especially if you want to for instance add services like live chat or transcoding. But even running more traditional services like modern e-mail servers with possible whistles and bells can be daunting. The same holds for deploying a VPN, private cloud storage, wiki, etc. Fediversity will enable all of these use cases, and more — finally bringing these to the market in a way that is as conveient as using a hosted service.

Fediversity will deliver an ambitious development effort, but this is a vast domain with many more challenges than what any preconceived effort could tackle by itself. This is why we invite your contribution to help us reshape the state of play, and together create an open, trustworthy and reliable internet for all.

This is your opportunity to make a real difference. You tell us how your project can help Fediversity go harder, better, faster, stronger. In order to enable you to make such contributions, we will award small to medium-size R&D grants towards solutions that bring the next generation of mobile solutions closer. We are seeking project proposals between 5.000 and 50.000 euro\'s — which should get you on your way. If the project works out well you can subsequently scale up in other programmes funded by NLnet such as NGI0 Commons Fund. Noteworthy fact: many projects which are to be deployed inside Fediversity were themselves bootstrapped on precisely such a grant from NGI, and now it is your turn.

']

The implementation of all selected projects will be structures into three phases over 36 months. Each phase will conclude with the submission of a report summarising the work completed during that phase:

Phase 1 – Early implementation and baseline phase (M1-M12): Upfront payment of 70% of the total fund (prefinancing); initial inception and engagement report (M12)

Phase 2 – Co-solution testing and assessment phase (M13-M24): Payment of 15% of the total fund; Intermediate progress report (M24)

Phase 3 – Final implementation phase (M25-M36): Payment of final 15% of the total fund; Final implementation report (M36)

Once submitted, each report will be reviewed by the Monitoring Committee. A request for revision may be issued with specific requirements for what should be modified. When the deliverable is officially accepted, payments will be released.

Selected applicants will cooperate closely with the Nemesis consortium and will be fully integrated into the co-creation and overall implementation processes. They will become active actors within their respective LLs and will benefit from the activities and knowledge generated by the project, while building capacity and co-implementing solutions at their experimental sites, contributing data and participating in the LL activities and initiatives.

The exact milestones, reporting obligations and payment conditions will be specified in the sub-grant agreement. Selected recipients will also be required to comply with obligations regarding conflict of interest, data sharing, FAIR principles, data protection, ethics, communication and dissemination, information obligations and record-keeping.

']

summary: NGI TALER open call (2026-08T)
content: NGI TALER open call (2026-08T)
citation: Pilots for the Next Generation Internet (IA). Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45635989
metadata.title: ['Pilots for the Next Generation Internet (IA)']
metadata.description:
['

In the digital economy, payments play a critical role. Yet online payment systems tend to allow for far less privacy than paying with a bank note or coins, especially when using proprietary solutions like Google Pay or Apple Pay. When interacting with the offline economy comes into play, the alternative of paying with all kind of volatile cryptocurrencies isn\'t a viable option either.


NGI TALER is a project funded by the European Commission and the Swiss State to roll out a new electronic payment system that benefits everyone: people, merchants, banks, financial authorities, auditors and anti-corruption researchers. The project doesn\'t have to start from scratch either, but builds on the strong foundations of GNU Taler — the privacy-preserving digital payment system developed by the GNU community and Taler Systems SA. This offers privacy for those that make payments, while enforcing transparency on those that sell. By providing micro payments at very low overhead, GNU Taler permits internet business models to shift away from advertising revenue or subscription models, especially for online publishers. No-risk transactions can lower transaction fees and open online payments for the underbanked population and citizens marginalized from digitalisation.


Part of the budget of NGI TALER (15% to be precise) is reserved for open calls to fund additional free and open source efforts that are aligned with the topics and approach of NGI TALER. We invite your contributions to help reshape the state of play of digital payment systems, and to help create an open, trustworthy and reliable internet for all. Of course you can contribute exciting new capabilities to GNU Taler itself, build auxiliary tools or work on user experience, but you could also be developing integrations into FOSS applications and open standards (enabling P2P micropayments in for instance an instant messenger, open social media platform or video conferencing tool), or work on improvements to infrastructure components like merchant backends. Together with the rest of the Next Generation Internet initiative, we move towards restoring and subsequently maintaining European sovereignty and to secure democratic ownership of our digital societies.


In order to enable you to make such contributions, we will award small to medium-size R&D grants towards technology commons that empower users, and establish a new generation of privacy-friendly digital payments systems. The programme will run between now and November 2026, but budgets are limited — so grab your opportunity.


Project results must be made available under a free and open source license, so anyone can read and validate the source code, and anyone can use the code to create technology that fits their own purposes. We are seeking project proposals between 5.000 and 50.000 euro\'s — if the project works out well you can subsequently scale up in other programmes funded by NLnet such as NGI0 Commons Fund. Reliable, low-cost, secure and resource efficient payments should become the \'new normal\' of the internet, something ordinary users should not have to worry about — users should be in control. So let\'s make it happen. Note that GNU Taler itself was funded through such Next Generation Internet projects, so there is every opportunity to grow.


Through the open calls, we assist independent researchers and developers to join in and create powerful new technologies and put these in the hands of future generations as building blocks for a fair and democratic society. Help us build a sustainable and open economy that benefits all, and submit your idea today. Not sure whether your idea fits? Don\'t be afraid to send something completely out-of-the-box if you think you can contribute to the topic (and it fits with the eligibility criteria): it really is an open call.


Want to learn more about Taler before you submit a proposal, or have other questions? Take a look at the GNU Taler technology, the main NGI Taler site - or join the GNU Taler Integration Community Hub to have a chat about your ideas.

']

content: RIS Education Open Call 2027
citation: EITURBANMOBILITY Business Plan 2026. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/48939761
metadata.title: ['EITURBANMOBILITY Business Plan 2026']
metadata.description:
['

The RIS Education Open Call 2027 aims at funding projects that can support the overall RIS strategy and aims, offer high-quality education, reach a large audience and market, and gain recognition efficiently with a lasting effect. The EIT Urban Mobility RIS Education objectives are focused on capacity building in innovation and entrepreneurship and urban mobility. These objectives are reflected in the activity types proposed in this call. The activities responding to these objectives should aim to improve transferrable skills that will lead to further innovation and entrepreneurship in urban mobility in RIS countries.   

There are five general topics (RISE 1-5) that can be addressed by the proposals: 

RISE1: Capacity-Building in Innovation and Entrepreneurship 

RISE2: Education and Training Activities in Urban Mobility 

RISE3: Organisation of Winter and Summer Schools 

RISE4: Scaling up of Local, Regional and National Initiatives 

RISE5: Network building with educational players 

']

summary: Master School and Fellowship Open Call 2027
content: Master School and Fellowship Open Call 2027
citation: EITURBANMOBILITY Business Plan 2026. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/48939761
metadata.title: ['EITURBANMOBILITY Business Plan 2026']
metadata.description:
['

EIT Urban Mobility invites universities, academic consortia, and education-focused innovators to submit proposals to its Master School and Fellowship Open Call 2027, designed to accelerate the development and delivery of high-quality learning experiences that build the skills and capabilities needed for sustainable urban mobility. 

This Call focuses on a series of specific activity areas aimed at meeting the objectives of the EIT Urban Mobility Master School and the EIT Urban Mobility Innovation & Entrepreneurship (I&E) Fellowship. The Call supports proposals that contribute to closing the knowledge gap within urban mobility by offering high-quality, challenge-based, intersectoral, and international education. 

We are seeking proposals that align with the key objectives of the Master School and I&E Fellowship, including fostering innovation and entrepreneurship skills, increasing student and graduate volumes, strengthening links to industry and cities, and supporting startup creation through EIT-labelled programmes. 

Proposals for the Master School and Fellowship Open Call 2027 may be submitted by individual applicants or consortia, depending on the requirements for each activity type, as detailed in the Call Manual. 

The following five types of activities are eligible under this Call: 

MSA1: Summer schools for Master students  

MSA2: New EIT Urban Mobility Master programmes  

MSA3: New universities joining the existing EIT Urban Mobility Master School programmes  

MSA4: I&E Master programmes that join the EIT Urban Mobility I&E Fellowship  

MSA5: Other educational activities contributing to KPI EITHE05.1 (startups created by students and graduates from EIT Labelled programmes) 

This Call supports activities that demonstrate clear alignment with the strategic objectives of EIT Urban Mobility’s education and training portfolio, ensure compliance with the EIT Label requirements and show strong potential for long-term impact, scalability, and financial sustainability. 

']

summary: COSMIC 2nd Open Call: AI & Data Solutions to Boost the Green Transition
content: COSMIC 2nd Open Call: AI & Data Solutions to Boost the Green Transition
citation: Industrial leadership in AI, Data and Robotics boosting competitiveness and the green transition (AI Data and Robotics Partnership) (IA)[[https://www.europarl.europa.eu/RegData/etudes/STUD/2021/662906/IPOL_STU(2021)662906_EN.pdf]]. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45704318
metadata.title: ['Industrial leadership in AI, Data and Robotics boosting competitiveness and the green transition (AI Data and Robotics Partnership) (IA)[[https://www.europarl.europa.eu/RegData/etudes/STUD/2021/662906/IPOL_STU(2021)662906_EN.pdf]]']
metadata.caName: ['COSMIC 2nd Open Call: AI & Data Solutions to Boost the Green Transition\n']
metadata.description:
['

The COSMIC project has received funding from the European Union’s Horizon Europe research and innovation programme under Grant Agreement No. 101189676.

COSMIC is supporting the Green Deal objectives by harnessing artificial intelligence (AI) and big data for large-scale energy resource optimisation. The project brings together a diverse consortium of large industries, research institutions, public authorities, NGOs, and SMEs to co-develop impactful, scalable digital solutions that address key sustainability challenges.

Through two Open Calls, COSMIC is selecting a maximum of 27 SMEs to co-develop AI-powered services and technologies aimed at enhancing energy efficiency and resource optimisation.

The 2nd Open Call will support up to 13 SMEs, including start-ups, working on AI- and data-driven innovations aligned with COSMIC’s mission. Selected participants will join a 10-month support programme offering mentoring, solution development, pilot site integration, and business scaling support.

Each selected SME may receive up to €150,000 funding as a fixed lump sum. Funding conditions include:

  1. SMEs and start-ups can receive reimbursement of up to 70% of their eligible costs.
  2. Non-profit legal entities may be funded up to 100% of their eligible costs.

Beneficiaries selected under this Open Call will have the opportunity to collaborate with COSMIC’s Core Technical Facilitators (CTFs) and Core Social Facilitator (CFS), receiving technological support to integrate their AI- and data-driven solutions. These solutions may be validated within COSMIC’s large-scale pilots across six European countries: Spain, Belgium, Portugal, France, Ireland, and Finland. Selected proposals will be supported for real-world testing in industrial or urban environments.

Each selected entity will develop a solution addressing one of the specific challenges outlined in the COSMIC Technical Guidelines. These include but are not limited to: carbon footprint integration, community engagement, waste classification, and smart energy management.

Participation is open to individual SMEs (including startups) from EU Member States and Horizon Europe Associated Countries.

Proposals must be AI- and data-centric, with clear potential for scalability, integration into COSMIC’s infrastructure, and measurable environmental and social impact.

']

summary: FS4Africa Open Call 2 (OC2) addressed to innovation hubs
content: FS4Africa Open Call 2 (OC2) addressed to innovation hubs
citation: EU-Africa Union – food safety. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45653635
metadata.title: ['EU-Africa Union – food safety']
metadata.caName: ['FS4Africa Open Call 2 (OC2) addressed to innovation hubs']
metadata.description:
['

The implementation of the FS4Africa sub-projects will be divided in 3 phases: Design (M1), Development (M2-M6), and Validation (M7-M8 months). Each of the 3 phases will be completed with submission of a deliverable, which summarises the work done during that phase. The review process will follow each phase of the implementation. Once the deliverable is received, it will be assessed by the FS4Africa Open Call Advisory Board members. A request for the deliverable\'s update may occur with specific requirements for what should be modified. Based on the results of the review and acceptance of respected deliverables, payment will be made towards the subgrantees in the following modalities:

  1. Phase 1 - Design (1 month / February 2027: Payment of 20% of the total grant [Deliverable: Training Activity plan];
  2. Phase 2 - Development (5 months / March 2027 – July 2027): Payment of 50% of the total grant [Deliverable: Training Delivery Report];
  3. Phase 3 - Validation (2 months / August 2027 – September 2027): Payment of the final 30% of the total grant [Deliverable: Final Report of the training activities].

Each Open Call 2 proposal must propose and implement a structured programme consisting of at least six (6) training–mentoring activities during the implementation period.

The required activities must include:

A. Support to Open Call 1 (OC1) Beneficiaries

Applicants must deliver at least two (2) training activities to support at least two (2) distinct Open Call 1 beneficiaries i.e. sub-projects (minimum two trainings in total, joint or dedicated sessions permitted). The two activities must involve at least two different OC1 sub-projects (i.e. not both activities mapped to the same OC1 beneficiary). The content should be tailored to the needs of the supported OC1 sub-projects and aligned with the relevant food safety challenges.

B. Support to FS4Africa Use Cases

Applicants must deliver at least four (4) training–mentoring activities targeting actors involved in one or more FS4Africa Use Cases in collaboration with other OC2 projects. Following selection, Open Call 2 sub-projects will coordinate among themselves, with support from the FS4Africa Executive Board (WP Leaders) and the Open Call team, through structured information exchange and alignment meetings to ensure balanced thematic coverage across all four FS4Africa Use Cases. Collaboration among OC2 sub-projects implies structured coordination of thematic focus, training design, delivery schedules, or mentoring approaches to ensure complementarity, avoid duplication, and maximise collective coverage of FS4Africa Use Cases.

Training–mentoring activities may take the form of online courses, webinars, interactive workshops, mentoring clinics, or other structured capacity-building formats. Joint or co-organised activities between OC2 sub-projects are optional but encouraged. Where such joint activities are implemented, they may count towards the minimum number (at least four) of required training–mentoring activities for each participating sub-project, provided that each sub-project’s role and contribution are clearly defined.

']

metadata.description:
['

The AID4SME - Open Call #2 is looking for Technology Developers to develop, test and validate Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Data solutions in the framework of the AID4SME project.

Applicants must submit their proposals to one of the following challenges:

  1. C1.1 Augmented sensing solution
  2. C2.1 Product-production Digital Twins
  3. C2.2 Automated monitoring hygiene inspection for refurbishment
  4. C2.3 Energy system Digital Twin decision support tool
  5. C3.2 Automated machine selection for parts production
  6. C3.3 Battery production digital work instructions & skill capturing
  7. C3.4 Automated warehouse and internal logistics management
  8. C4.1 Smart energy management of unplanned machine downtime
  9. C4.2 Semi-automated EV battery disassembly for recycling
  10. C4.3 Co-bot refrigerator door assembly solutions

The AID4SME Programme #2 is organised in 4 stages of 14 months. Stage 1 - Plan from 01 November 2026 to 31 November 2026, Stage 2 - Development from 01 December 2026 to 30 June 2027, Stage 3 - Test from 01 July 2027 to 31 August 2027, and Stage 4 - Assessment from 01 September 2027 to 31 December 2027.

During these phases, beneficiaries will have the support of a mentor to monitor project progress and facilitate communication with technical partners.

The programme will select up to 12 applications, each addressing one of the challenges/topics defined in Section 3 - Challenges of the Annex 1. Guidelines for Applicants, providing a maximum contribution of EUR 200 000.00, to be paid in form of lump sums at the end of Periods 1, 2 and 3 (30% + 40% + 30%) (section7.1.2).

citation: Specialised education programmes or modules in key capacity areas. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45114064
metadata.title: ['Specialised education programmes or modules in key capacity areas']
metadata.caName: ['Cyberactioning FSTP Call – Graduation Week Mobility Grants 2026 (MICAC Cohort 1)']
metadata.description:
['

The Master in International Cybersecurity and Cyberintelligence (MICAC) is a joint Master’s programme delivered within the Arqus European University Alliance, involving the University of Granada, the University of Minho, the University of Padua and Vilnius University. The programme combines advanced academic training with international mobility, aiming to equip students with specialised skills in cybersecurity and cyberintelligence.

Within this framework, the MICAC Graduation Week constitutes a key academic milestone of the programme. It brings together students, academic staff and partners for a week of intensive activities, including thesis-related work, final academic exercises and programme-level events.

Participation in the Graduation Week requires the physical presence of students at the University of Minho, where the activities are hosted. This mobility component is an essential part of the academic structure of the programme and contributes to the achievement of its learning outcomes.

The present FSTP scheme aims to support this mobility by providing financial assistance to students, facilitating their participation in the Graduation Week and ensuring equal access to this key programme activity, in line with the objectives of the Cyberactioning project and the Digital Europe Programme.

This call (as well as the financial support that will be provided through it) is NOT an EU action. This call is launched solely by Universoty of Minho. The financial support to the selected successful applicants will be provided directly by University of Minho in its own name and under its own responsibility. University of Minho’s own responsibility includes the preparation and drafting of this call as well as the selection of successful applicants that will receive the said financial support. The publication of this call in the EU Funding & Tenders Portal is made solely for information purposes with the aim to increase the visibility of this call to the general public, including to potential applicants (i.e. students). The said publication shall not be construed as direct or indirect approval of the content and conditions of this call by the relevant EU authorities or as any form of waiver from the above-described responsibility of University of Minho.

']

summary: Third Call for Pilots Work Strand 1 - Connecting Existing Local Digital Twins
content: Third Call for Pilots Work Strand 1 - Connecting Existing Local Digital Twins
citation: Towards networked Local Digital Twins in the EU. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/47038104
metadata.title: ['Towards networked Local Digital Twins in the EU']
metadata.caName: ['Third Call for Pilots Work Strand 1 - Connecting Existing Local Digital Twins']
metadata.description:
['

Task description

The Local Digital Twins for Smart Sustainable Cities (LDT4SSC) initiative is launching an Open Call for Pilots to engage European local and regional authorities, as well as a wide range of stakeholders, including public administrations at local, regional, and national levels, businesses, technology developers, suppliers, research institutions, and academia, in building a networked ecosystem of Local Digital Twins (LDTs).

The third call concerns Work Strand 1, 2 and 3 (WS1, WS2 and WS3), of which WS1 aims to interconnect existing LDTs and create a federated EU-wide network supporting seamless data exchange and interoperability. WS1 focuses on federation and interoperability, requiring pilots to prioritise cross-border data exchange and the creation of shared data spaces, demanding a baseline of digital maturity and collaboration among multiple public authorities.


Who Can Apply

The open call is designed to engage a diverse range of local and regional communities. Participation is open to public administrations at local, regional, and national levels, including European Digital Infrastructure Consortia (EDICs) such as the LDT CitiVERSE EDIC, businesses, technology developers, suppliers, research institutions, academia, as well as non-governmental organizations, non-profit organizations, and other research partners or civil society actors.

To ensure the replicability and scalability of each pilot, every consortium is required to include at least one additional partner from the private sector, such as a service provider, private association with legal status, trusted third party, or a private representative of a use-case sector.

Members of the LDT4SSC Consortium are not permitted to join pilot consortia.

All partners must be based in eligible countries and be able to co-finance at least 50% of the project costs.


Consortia Eligibility Criteria

  1. Each pilot consortium must comprise at least two local and regional authorities drawn from two different eligible countries. Eligible countries include EU Member States (including outermost regions and overseas territories), Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein, and listed EEA and DEP Associated countries.
  2. To ensure the replicability and scalability of the pilot, each consortium is encouraged to involve at least one additional partner from one of the following categories: private entity (e.g., service provider), private association with legal status, trusted third party, or private representative of a use-case sector.
  3. Security restrictions: Consortium members must not be controlled by an ineligible country. If there is a potential risk, entities may provide a guarantee.
  4. Ownership Control Declaration (OCD) requirements: All entities except those validated as public bodies by EU countries must submit an OCD. It must be self-declared at proposal submission and, if the pilot is awarded, the official OCD must be provided within 14 days of notification. If a consortium member is controlled by an ineligible country, participation will be conditional on submission and acceptance of an adequate guarantee.


Scope

WS1 is dedicated to connecting and federating LDTs across cities, communities and regions to create a unified, interoperable and reusable EU LDT ecosystem. In doing so, WS1 will support communities in accessing a comprehensive set of technical open specifications and open-source software components to interconnect their LDTs through Data Spaces, with most solutions expected to align with the SIMPL framework where appropriate. These efforts are expected to draw on the European Data Space for Smart and Sustainable Cities and Communities (DS4SSCC) as well as relevant thematic data spaces such as those for tourism, mobility and other domains, with which data exchange is envisaged.

By leveraging standards, pilots will break down silos, scale up EU-wide datasets and onboard less-advanced communities into the LDT network. The goal is hence to enhance decision-making (particularly in key EU policies (like mobility, energy efficiency, or environmental), reduce redundancies and foster a collaborative data economy, ultimately laying the foundation for a resilient, interconnected digital infrastructure that supports Europe’s green and digital ambitions.

Through real-world use cases, such as cross-border traffic management or multi-city resource optimisation, it is expected that WS1 will transform fragmented local initiatives into a cohesive, replicable, scalable and reusable federation of LDTs, driving collective innovation and efficiency.


Available funding and Co-Financing

The EU contribution for WS1 under the third call amounts to €2,376,355.00, to be distributed across approximately 2-3 selected consortia. The co-funding model ensures that EU support covers up to 50% of eligible expenses, with applicants demonstrating the capacity to finance at least 50% of pilot costs from their own resources.

Key financial rules:

  1. The maximum grant per third-party (across multiple consortia and awarded pilot projects) is €500,000. Any expenses exceeding this amount must be fully covered by consortium members.
  2. The maximum cumulative grant per consortium is €1,000,000.
  3. Applicants must cover at least 50% of total pilot costs.

Co-financing must be a dedicated, auditable cost, explicitly included in the pilot budget and documented according to normal accounting practices. Only direct monetary contributions or monetized resources listed in the budget are eligible; in-kind contributions not monetized in the budget are not admissible.

All costs must be actual, incurred during the pilot period, and fully compliant with applicable regulations, including state aid rules and prevention of double funding.

Participating local and regional administrations, as well as any other consortium members contributing financially, must demonstrate their capacity to provide at least 50% co-financing through a Letter of Commitment.

The pilot consortium will receive 30% of the total grant as pre-financing upon signing the Grant Agreement, with the remaining 70% paid in two instalments based on performance during the midterm and final reporting periods.

For more information, please refer to the relevant Call for Pilots Manual at https://ldt4ssc.eu/opencalls/


Additional information

We invite applicants to be part of the Stakeholder Forum to engage and network with likeminded organisations aiming to realise the objectives for LDT4SSC. This forum also offers opportunities to directly liaise with the LDT4SSC consortium members. More information at https://ldt4ssc.eu/

Applicants are responsible for checking the LDT4SSC project website (https://ldt4ssc.eu/opencalls/) for updated information related to this Call, including the information about the schedule for Info Sessions.


For any inquiries, please contact info@ldt4ssc.eu.

Project details

Project acronym

LDT4SSC

Full name of the EU funded project

Local Digital Twins for Smart and Sustainable Communities

Grant agreement number

101226211

Topic

DIGITAL-2024-CLOUD-DATA-AI-07-DIGITALTWIN - Towards networked Local Digital Twins in the EU

Programme

DIGITAL

']

WS2 focuses on creating new Local Digital Twins (LDTs) that address shared community needs. Each pilot will deliver practical LDT services and ready‑to‑use tools showing how shared data, common data models, and reusable software can work together to create real benefits for communities.

  1. The maximum grant per third-party (across multiple consortia and awarded pilot projects) is €500,000. Any expenses exceeding this amount must be fully covered by consortium members.

  3. Applicants must cover at least 50% of total pilot costs.

Only direct monetary contributions or monetized resources listed in the budget are eligible; in-kind contributions not monetized in the budget are not admissible.

All costs must be actual, incurred during the pilot period, and fully compliant with applicable regulations, including state aid rules and prevention of double funding.

Participating local and regional administrations, as well as any other consortium members contributing financially, must demonstrate their capacity to provide at least 50% co-financing through a Letter of Commitment.

The pilot consortium will receive 30% of the total grant as pre-financing upon signing the Grant Agreement, with the remaining 70% paid in two instalments based on performance during the midterm and final reporting periods.

For more information, please refer to the relevant Call for Pilots Manual at https://ldt4ssc.eu/opencalls/


Additional information

We invite applicants to be part of the Stakeholder Forum to engage and network with likeminded organisations aiming to realise the objectives for LDT4SSC. This forum also offers opportunities to directly liaise with the LDT4SSC consortium members. More information at https://ldt4ssc.eu/

Applicants are responsible for checking the LDT4SSC project website (https://ldt4ssc.eu/opencalls/) for updated information related to this Call, including the information about the schedule for Info Sessions.


For any inquiries, please contact info@ldt4ssc.eu.

content: Open Call 1: Penetration Testing Scenarios Development
citation: Preparedness Support and Mutual Assistance, Targeting Larger Industrial Operations and Installations. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/47039365
metadata.title: ['Preparedness Support and Mutual Assistance, Targeting Larger Industrial Operations and Installations']
metadata.description:
['

The CIRCAT 18-Month Support Programme

Upon selection and signature of the Sub-Grant Agreement, beneficiaries will receive up to €40,000 in funding, disbursed across a 6-month tailored Support Programme. Each beneficiary will be paired with a dedicated CIRCAT expert mentor and guided through four operational stages:

Stage 1: Individual Mentoring Plan (Up to 1 month). Definition of a structured work plan tailored to the project. The Individual Mentoring Plan (IMP), a document that establishes the project\'s specific key performance indicators (KPIs) and deliverables.

Stage 2: Scenario Definition (Up to 3 months). Identification of the relevant threat vectors and detailing the technical steps, tools, and methodologies required to safely replicate these threats in a controlled environment.

Stage 3: Scenario Integration and Validation (Up to 2 months). The Mentors collaborate with participants to validate the scenarios, ensuring they accurately reflect emerging attack patterns and exploit techniques.

CIRCAT OC1 beneficiaries have to design a Penetration Testing Scenario, following the template provided in Annex 1, in one of the CIRCAT prioritised verticals (Energy, Health, Public Administration, Digital infrastructure, ICT Service management (B2B) and Financial Entities (DORA)).

']

summary: Scaling Green Innovation with Industry – Growth Box Call
content: Scaling Green Innovation with Industry – Growth Box Call
citation: Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/47108154
metadata.title: ['Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery']
metadata.caName: ['Scaling Green Innovation with Industry – Growth Box Call']
metadata.description:
['

Under the Growth Box stream, the eligible activity is the validation, demonstration, and scaling of an innovative solution addressing one of the predefined challenges identified by GreenGrid and described in Chapter 16 of the Call document. The objective is to test, validate, and advance the solution under real operational or market conditions, contributing to grid flexibility, renewable integration, digitalisation, or overall energy system resilience.

The proposed solution must already be developed and demonstrate sufficient maturity for real-environment testing (recommended TRL 5–8). Activities focused purely on early-stage research or concept development (TRL 1–4) are not eligible.

Eligible activities may include:

·      Adaptation and technical refinement of the solution to the specific challenge context;

·       Pilot implementation or demonstration in collaboration with the Challenge Owner;

·       Integration, testing, and validation in an operational environment;

·       Performance monitoring, impact measurement, and data analysis;

·       Preparation for market uptake and scaling within the Challenge Owner’s ecosystem or sector.

The activity must demonstrate clear added value compared to the current state of practice and show potential for replication or broader market deployment. The scope of activities must be feasible within the proposed budget and the defined project duration.

']

summary: Open Call for Farmers to participate in the TRAILS4SOIL Project
content: Open Call for Farmers to participate in the TRAILS4SOIL Project
citation: Co-creating solutions for soil health in Living Labs. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46597762
metadata.title: ['Co-creating solutions for soil health in Living Labs']
metadata.caName: ['Open Call for Farmers to participate in the TRAILS4SOIL Project']
metadata.description:
['

Farmers\' commitment

It is expected that the selected farmers commit to the following conditions:

  1. Provide a field of at least 1 hectare for the implementation of the ReCAP practices.
  2. Provide relevant information and data required for the evaluation and monitoring of the activities.
  3. Discuss and agree with the leaders of the TRAILS4SOIL Living Labs, which practices will be applied in accordance with the Project’s ReCAP list, and maintain the agreed practices throughout the duration of the project.
  4. Collaborate with the TRAILS4SOIL Living Lab leaders to collect soil health and socio-economic monitoring data and provide updates/feedback on the implementation of the ReCAP practices in order to adapt project activities where necessary.
  5. If applicable, collaborate with selected companies specialised in Monitoring, Reporting and Verification (MRV) to test their technology and provide related data.
  6. Participate in person in workshops or meetings organised by the Living Labs to exchange practices and experiences.
  7. If selected as a lighthouse site (i.e. sites designated to demonstrate good practices), lead field visits for training, peer learning and communication purposes.
  8. Selected farmers will be required to sign a dedicated Sub-Grant Agreement (SGA) with the Trails4Soil coordinator to fulfil the legal and administrative requirements governing the relationship between the Trails4Soil consortium and each beneficiary of the Open Call.
']

metadata.description:
['

The MAT4EU Programme of MAT4EU Innovation Open Call will offer a range of complementary services to selected SMEs. It aims to generate impact beyond the individual results of funded SME projects by combining financial support with a structured set of complementary services. While SME projects are expected to deliver specific technological and business outcomes, the Programme, as a whole, contributes to a broader impact at European level by:

• Strengthening the innovation capacity of SMEs in advanced materials

• Accelerating the adoption and industrial deployment of innovative solutions

• Enhancing collaboration across regions, sectors, and value chains

• Facilitating SMEs’ access to international markets

• Supporting the green and digital transition of European industry

More information can be found on the Guide for Applicants of the MAT4EU Innovation Open Call.

']

metadata.description:
['

The procedure follows the principles of:

  1. transparency,
  2. equal treatment,
  3. confidentiality,
  4. absence of conflict of interest.

Admissibility and eligibility checks precede scoring; only eligible applications will be scored.

Eligible proposals are ranked by total score within the available budget.

The final award decision is endorsed by POLI.design. All applicants will receive a written justification for the decision, with terms to appeal.

Selected beneficiaries will be requested to sign a Financial Support Agreement specifying the supported activity, eligible costs, maximum amount, payment schedule and reporting obligations.

Failure to sign within the indicated timeframe may lead to loss of the grant by the autonomous decision of POLI.design.

Payments will be made by POLI.design, normally after participation in the activity and approval of the beneficiary report and cost statement. Details are provided in the individual Financial Support Agreement (including e.g. the possibility of small prefinancing in justified cases).

']

summary: GUARDIANS Test Before Invest Open Call
content: GUARDIANS Test Before Invest Open Call
citation: Smart solutions for the use of digital technologies for small- and medium-sized, farms and farm structures. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/44095153
metadata.title: ['Smart solutions for the use of digital technologies for small- and medium-sized, farms and farm structures']
metadata.description:
['

The European project GUARDIANS aims to empower small - and medium-sized farms through smart solutions, bridging the technological gap with larger food producers. GUARDIANS will put in place a set of cost-effective and human-centric digital solutions, which aim to support and empower the penetration, sustainability and resilience of more agroecological farming models, like regenerative agriculture, natural grazing, alternative managed grazing strategies, and the promotion of pollinators in different farm structures. Following a multi-actor co-creation methodology 9 different technologies, combining software and hardware, will be customized to cover the farmers\' needs.

To reinforce multi-actor collaborations and foster co-creation between research and technical experts and farmers and boost the overall impact of the project, GUARDIANS will launch two Open Calls.

The first one, the Co-Creation Open Call, was launched on May 2025 and selected 9 consortia, each consisting of one tech provider and one farm, to co-design, develop and test technological solutions. This Open Call had a budget of €729,000.

The second one, the Test Before Invest Open Call, will select up to 9 farms to test and validate up to 9 technological solutions. These solutions will be selected from both the 9 technologies developed during the Co-Creation Open Call and those 9 emerging from the project itself, with a budget of €90,000 (up to €10,000 per farm). During the execution of the Test Before Invest Open Call, the entity in charge of the technological solution will support the applicant throughout the testing process.

Beneficiaries from GUARDIANS Test Before Invest Open Call will participate in a 3-month funding programme, focused on replicating and testing digital technologies developed under the GUARDIANS project or the Co-Creation Open Call.

The beneficiaries will have the following responsibilities:

  1. Implement the selected technology according to the instructions provided by the technology provider. (Milestone 1)
  2. Provide feedback and basic KPI data to the technology provider via short forms or questionnaires. (Milestone 2)
  3. Share lessons learned or suggestions for improving the technology in real-world farm conditions. (Milestone 3)

Funding is lump-sum based, up to €10.000 per farm, disbursed upon successful completion of the milestones. While farms do not produce full deliverables themselves, they are responsible for providing the required KPI forms, installation confirmation and qualitative feedback.

']

summary: VIC FSTP - ISTEC Summer School Scholarships 2025-2026
content: VIC FSTP - ISTEC Summer School Scholarships 2025-2026
citation: Specialised Education Programmes in Key Capacity Areas. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46813062
metadata.title: ['Specialised Education Programmes in Key Capacity Areas']
metadata.description:
['

This call provides Financial Support to Third Parties (FSTP) in the form of institutional scholarships to support participation in the 2025-2026 edition of the VIC Summer School organised by ISTEC.

The objective of the call is to reduce financial barriers to access and to promote diversity, inclusion, and participation in advanced digital skills education in the fields of immersive technologies and artificial intelligence.

Through this call, selected participants may receive partial financial support applied directly to the participation fee of the programme.

The support is intended to facilitate access for participants who may otherwise face economic or structural barriers to participation, particularly those from underrepresented groups, interdisciplinary or non-ICT backgrounds, and applicants contributing to the diversity and inclusion objectives of the programme.

This call contributes to the broader objectives of the Virtual Innovation Consortium (VIC), namely the strengthening of European advanced digital skills capacity in Virtual Reality (VR), Augmented Reality (AR), Extended Reality (XR), and related digital innovation domains.

']

content: POLITO GreenChips-EDU Scholarship
citation: Specialised education programmes or modules in key capacity areas. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45114064
metadata.title: ['Specialised education programmes or modules in key capacity areas']
metadata.description:
['

APPENDIX

SCORE CALCULATION

1. Criteria for Master’s degree programmes

The score for the students enrolled in a Master’s degree programme is determined based on the following criteria:

  1. Politecnico di Torino takes into consideration only the exam credits included in the StudyPlan which count towards the achievement of the degree and are validated by Politecnico di Torino;
  2. exams passed by the applicants but not validated are not counted (i.e., following a transfer);
  3. additional credits do not count towards the score (i.e., additional credits of Percorso Intraprendenti, Italian language credits for international students, traineeship extension credits, credits awarded following participation in special events/activities, etc.);
  4. in case of FULL TIME or PART-TIME enrolment for an entire academic year, the number of semesters of the career duration is set at 2;
  5. in case of an equal score on the ranking list, the younger applicant will be chosen;
  6. Politecnico di Torino takes into consideration the so called “integrated courses” (corsi integrati) exclusively if the student has taken all modules of the course, even if not registered in the transcript yet;
  7. pass-or-fail exams (exams without grade) are given the value of the average grade of other exams passed by the applicant up to the present.

Please note that for all courses/activities that can be recorded in the student transcript in a period of the year other than examination sessions (i.e., traineeship, external training activities…) Politecnico di Torino takes into consideration the actual registration date; therefore, if these activities are recorded after February 21, 2026 they are not considered for the score.

Students with a particularly complex university career are invited to contact the International Mobility Unit through the Ticketing Support System when they submit their application (topic “MOBILITÀ OUTGOING – TESI SU PROPOSTA”. Tickets sent after the deadlines for application submission will not be accepted for the calculation of the score.


2. Score calculation – Master’s degree programmes

Each student will be assigned a score calculated as follows:

(GR1 * CFU1) + (GR2 * CFU2) + (GRn * CFUn)

number of semesters from the student\'s first enrolment in the university system

GR= grade obtained in the exam

CFU= number of credits of the exam

(pass-or-fail exams are given the value of the average grade of the other exams)

If some credits are recognized following student forfeiture or withdrawal, the number of semesters is counted as specified in Section 2.1.

However, in the event of a transfer from another Italian university, semesters are counted from the date of first enrolment in the university system, even if the student did not get any exam validated or any credit transfer for the attainment of the degree.

The maximum number of credits used to calculate the score is 120 CFU, total number of credits required to earn the Master’s degree.

For eligible applicants, the merit score will be calculated according to the following formula:

Score = sum (Master’s exam grade * CFU credit) / N. examination semesters

Each applicant is assigned a score calculated by multiplying the number of credits of each passed exam by its grade; the sum of these products is divided by the number of semesters since the first enrolment in a Master’s degree programme at any Italian or foreign university and then rounded up. The number of semesters of the Master’s degree for which the applicant has advanced some Master’s courses is evaluated in semesters starting from the semester in which the applicant has added the first Master’s course in his/her Annual Personal Study Plan.


2.1 Calculation of the score in case of credit transfer (between degree programmes of the same level) and credits recognition after withdrawal, forfeiture and dual enrolment – career duration

In the event of credit transfer (between degree programmes of the same level) and in case of credit recognition after withdrawal, forfeiture and dual enrolment, the exams recorded in the study plan after their validation make the number of semesters increase (depending on the number of recognized credits) as shown in the table below:

3 RECOGNIZED CREDITS

ADDITIONAL SEMESTERS

1 - 30

1 semester

31 - 60

2 semesters

61 - 90

3 semesters

91 - 120

4 semesters

from 121

5 semesters


2.2 Unregistered exams

Applicants who are waiting for the registration of some exams in their transcript when they submit their application must report this situation during the application phase using the online form that can be downloaded from the Bando Tesi su proposta website.

In particular Politecnico di Torino accepts self-declarations by:

  1. ongoing mobility students, or students who have completed a mobility project, students who have passed exams abroad by February 21, 2026, without having yet obtained the exam validation by Politecnico by the same date;
  2. students who have completed a mobility project, students who have passed exams abroad by February 21, 2026, and these exams have been validated by Politecnico di Torino at a later date;
  3. students who have passed exams at Politecnico di Torino by February 21, 2026, still waiting for the exam registration in the transcript by Politecnico by the same date.

The exams passed during a mobility project and self-certified by the student are assigned an evaluation (grade out of 30) equal to the weighted average of the other exams recorded in the student’s transcript by February 21, 2026.

In the self-declaration students must indicate:

  1. for exams taken during the mobility project: the number of credits of the corresponding exams provided in the study plan as set out in the last approved Learning Agreement;
  2. for exams passed at Politecnico di Torino that have not been registered in the transcript yet: course denomination, grade and exam date.
']

summary: JARVIS Open Call #2 - External Pilots Track
content: JARVIS Open Call #2 - External Pilots Track
citation: Industrial leadership in AI, Data and Robotics – advanced human robot interaction (AI Data and Robotics Partnership) (IA). Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45634882
metadata.title: ['Industrial leadership in AI, Data and Robotics – advanced human robot interaction (AI Data and Robotics Partnership) (IA)']
metadata.description:
['

The External Pilots OC2 track aims to validate innovative AI-enabled solutions in real industrial environments (TRL7) and validate the JARVIS modules in new application domains. Applicants are invited to submit proposals addressing one of the following challenges:

  1. Worker-Centric Collaborative Robotics Applications for Physically Demanding Tasks in Manufacturing Environments
  2. Adaptive Human–Robot Interaction in Complex Manufacturing Processes
  3. Remote Human-Robot Interaction for Inspection and Maintenance of Plants, Infrastructure Areas and Linear Infrastructure
  4. Inspection and Maintenance of Industrial and Public Sector Assets and Equipment
  5. Human–Robot Interaction in Emerging and Strategic Domains

Selected projects are expected to:

  1. Design and implement a pilot solution addressing one of the five JARVIS challenges
  2. Integrate one JARVIS module into their system via ROS2-compatible interfaces
  3. Deploy and validate the solution in a real operational environment provided by the Use Case Provider
  4. Demonstrate the added value of JARVIS technologies in improving existing processes, such as productivity, flexibility, safety, or human-robot collaboration

The work will be structured in three Sprints:

  1. Sprint 1 – Design: ·Detailed definition of the system architecture, integration approach with the selected JARVIS module, demonstration scenarios, validation strategy, and performance indicators.
  2. Sprint 2 – Development & Deployment: Development of the solution and initial deployment in the pilot environment
  3. Sprint 3 – Integration & Validation: Full integration, testing, and validation in real conditions

Applicants are expected to deliver:

  1. A functional pilot demonstrating the proposed solution (TRL7)
  2. Validation results based on defined KPIs
  3. Documentation of integration with JARVIS components
  4. A clear pathway for further exploitation and scalability

All solutions must be compatible with the JARVIS objectives and contribute to demonstrating its applicability in new industrial contexts.

']

summary: Open Call for innovative projects enhancing the valorisation of agri-food biomass
content: Open Call for innovative projects enhancing the valorisation of agri-food biomass
citation: Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/47108154
metadata.title: ['Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery']
metadata.caName: ['Open Call for innovative projects enhancing the valorisation of agri-food biomass']
metadata.description:
['

This call targets innovative SMEs developing solutions that increase the valorisation of biomass, contributing to more sustainable and resilient agri-food ecosystems through the development of new processes, services and products.

This Innovation Call includes financial support for projects especially focusing on the development, adoption, and commercialisation of innovative upcycled ingredients for food and non-food applications, products, business processes, and technologies (including digital) that maximise the valorisation of agri-food biomass and side-streams. These projects should have a minimum Technology Readiness Level (TRL) of 6.

1.The Innovation Open Call targets:

  1. SMEs in the agri-food production and/or processing sector;
  2. SMEs in other sectors (ex. cosmetics, nutraceuticals, etc) that valorise biomass/by-products/side-streams from the agri-food sector;
  3. SMEs in other sectors (ex. cosmetics, nutraceuticals, etc) that valorise their own biomass/by-products/side-streams for the agri-food sector;
  4. SMEs offering solutions and/or technology for the valorisation of biomass/by-products/side-streams from the agri-food sector (development and application of new technologies or processes).

2.The applicant’s project must lead to the implementation of either a new process, a new product, or a new service or a combination of them.

3.Proposals can be submitted either by a single SME or by an SMEs consortium with up to three (3) SMEs.

4.Applicants can be SMEs registered at EU Member States and countries associated to the Single Market Programme.

5.Projects must have a minimum Technology Readiness Level (TRL) of at least 6.

6.For Single SME projects, the maximum lump-sum grant per project (1 SME) is €60.000.

7.For collaborative projects, the maximum lump-sum grant per project (2-3 SMEs) is €90.000.

8.Innovation grants must be matched by at least 15% private financial contribution (debt or own financial resources), with proof required.

']

summary: Enhancing the Cybersecurity Capabilities of the Domestic SME Sector (SSNS-NCC-HU-FSTP-2026)
content: Enhancing the Cybersecurity Capabilities of the Domestic SME Sector (SSNS-NCC-HU-FSTP-2026)
citation: Deploying The Network of National Coordination Centres with Member States. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46786367
metadata.title: ['Deploying The Network of National Coordination Centres with Member States']
metadata.caName: ['Enhancing the Cybersecurity Capabilities of the Domestic SME Sector (SSNS-NCC-HU-FSTP-2026)']
metadata.description:
['

WHO CAN SUBMIT A GRANT APPLICATION?

Micro, small and medium-sized enterprises may submit grant applications for the call if they:

  1. have at least three closed financial years (each supported by financial statements and covering full 365-day period of operation as a pre-company is not included);
  2. operate in the sectors listed in Annexes 2 or 3 of the Cybersecurity Act

WHAT ACTIVITIES ARE ELIGIBLE FOR SUPPORT?

  1. Acquisition of new equipment and intangible assets resulting in the enhancement of cyber resilience
  2. Centralised endpoint protection solutions (traditional antivirus);
  3. Advanced endpoint protection and incident detection solutions (EDR/XDR);
  4. Perimeter protection solutions (firewall, Web Application Firewall, proxy, VPN gateway);
  5. Network traffic monitoring and intrusion detection solutions (WAF, IDS/IPS, NDR);
  6. Solutions against denial-of-service attacks (Anti-DDoS);
  7. Data backup and recovery solutions (backup/restore);
  8. Centralised log collection solutions (log management);
  9. Advanced log analysis solutions;
  10. Security information and event management solutions (SIEM);
  11. Mobile device management solutions (MDM);
  12. Multi-factor authentication solutions (MFA);
  13. Identity and access a management solutions (IAM);
  14. Data loss prevention solutions (DLP);
  15. Anti-spam and e-mail security solutions;
  16. Governance, risk and compliance management solutions (GRC);
  17. Encryption and key management solutions;
  18. Replacement of hardware or software components that are no longer supported by security update (obsolete hardware/software).
  19. Procurement of Services related to the enhancement of cyber resilience:
  20. Cybersecurity testing and assessment services (vulnerability testing);
  21. Cybersecurity Operations Centre (SOC) services;
  22. Cybersecurity-related training activities;
  23. Subscriptions to cybersecurity training platforms;
  24. Integration of hardware and software tools (system design, implementation, configuration, testing and documentation);
  25. Cybersecurity-related consulting and professional advisory services.

Details are included in the call for tenders!

']
